1	# Q1 Evaluation Thresholds
     2	
     3	This file defines the evaluation bar the recommender model must meet before
     4	any API change that touches the recommender surface can ship.
     5	
     6	## The Q1 eval set
     7	- Name: `q1-2026-eval-v3`
     8	- Size: 12,480 labeled queries
     9	- Frozen as of: 2026-01-15
    10	- Location: `s3://ml-evals/q1-2026-eval-v3/`
    11	
    12	## Thresholds (must all hold)
    13	- **Precision @ 10**: >= **0.85**
    14	- Recall @ 50: >= 0.72
    15	- Mean latency p95: <= 180 ms
    16	
    17	## Interpretation
    18	If precision drops below 0.85 on this eval set, block the release. This is
    19	the single hard gate. Recall and latency are informational — log them, flag
    20	regressions, but do not block on them.
    21	
    22	## Process
    23	- Platform should call `just eval recommender` before deploying any change
    24	  touching `api` that references recommender code paths.
    25	- Results are written to `s3://ml-evals/results/` and linked in the deploy PR.

blocks.text: I have Q2. Now I need platform deploy info. I don't see a `platform` directory under teams. Let me check if there's a runbook somewhere.

tool_calls.result:
1	# ML Team — Agent Guide
     2	
     3	Context for LLM agents acting on behalf of the ML team.
     4	
     5	## Who we are
     6	ML team owns the recommender model, the eval harness, and the offline
     7	training pipeline. We do not own any user-facing services.
     8	
     9	## Key files in our team memory
    10	- `evaluations/q1.md` — Q1 eval thresholds and the current eval set definition.
    11	
    12	## Overrides we maintain (vs. org defaults)
    13	- Python version floor: **3.11** (matches Platform).
    14	- We pin `numpy<2.0` because our training image hasn't migrated yet.
    15	
    16	## Cross-team coordination
    17	- Before Platform deploys an API change touching the recommender surface,
    18	  they must confirm our precision threshold is met. See `evaluations/q1.md`.
    19	
    20	## House style
    21	- Report metrics to 3 decimal places.
    22	- Never include raw user IDs in commits, even in test fixtures.
